prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Pathfinder Golarion inspired epic political fantasy RPG setting. Terrain type: dual_continent. Draw two large fictional continents in illustrated Pathfinder aged ink-on-parchment cartography, separated by a dramatic non-vertical Inner Sea passage: diagonal and S-shaped straits, crescent gulfs, political sea lanes, shoals, broken coastal shelves, and twisting channels rather than a simple straight split. One continent contains sprawling harbor city-states, Absalom-like island metropolis, ancient god-war ruins, magical schools, forest nations, mountain citadels, and trade roads; the other contains desert empires, jungle interiors, northern frozen wastes, haunted necromantic realms, volcanic badlands, nomad steppes, and Pathfinder expedition routes. Complex realistic coastlines on both continents: peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands, sea caves. Islands are unevenly scattered between and around the continents with varied size and shape, including independent city-state islands, ruin chains, pirate coves, temple isles, and monster-haunted rocks. Large detailed political fantasy atlas, subtle region tinting and heraldic-style markers allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
